compose_file: compose.yml
main_service: server
main_port: 3000
preferred_slugs: [twenty, crm, twenty-crm]

# /healthz is Twenty's cheap liveness route (the upstream compose probes it too).
# First boot runs the NestJS server's schema migrations against the freshly
# provisioned managed Postgres before it listens, which on a cold image pull can
# take a while — allow a generous start period.
health_probe:
  path: /healthz
  start_period: 180s

# Twenty's own docs set a 2 GB-RAM floor for the stack; server + worker share it.
resources:
  recommended: { memory: 2G, cpu: 1.0 }

# The CRM's records (contacts, companies, opportunities, custom objects) live in
# the managed Postgres database — backed up by the managed-service path, not
# attributed here. The only app bind is ./data/local-storage: Twenty's
# STORAGE_TYPE=local attachment store (files uploaded onto records), shared by
# the server and worker. It has no use-case-folder home and Twenty offers no
# external-library path for it, so it is app-managed content — this forfeits the
# files-first-class badge and triggers the install-time warning.
# estimated_size: the attachment bind is near-empty at first-healthy (measured on
# a clean boot); growth is user-uploaded attachments, which is runtime usage and
# deliberately not projected here.
storage:
  data_volumes: [./data/local-storage]
  estimated_size: 4MB
  app_managed_user_content: true

# Postgres holds all CRM data; Redis backs the BullMQ job queue the worker drains
# and the server's caches. The brain provisions both per-app in its shared managed
# instances and injects MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_* / MALMO_SERVICE_CACHE_*, which the
# compose maps onto Twenty's PG_DATABASE_URL and REDIS_URL DSNs. `redis: "7"`
# normalizes to the BSD-3 Valkey 8 engine (RESP-compatible).
services:
  database:
    type: postgres
    version: "16"
  cache:
    type: redis
    version: "7"

# Twenty requires ENCRYPTION_KEY — it encrypts every secret stored in the database
# (OAuth tokens, app variables, TOTP secrets); losing it loses access to them. The
# brain generates it once at install and injects it as MALMO_SECRET_ENCRYPTION_KEY,
# stable across restarts so existing data stays decryptable.
secrets:
  - name: encryption_key

# internet: Twenty fetches company favicons/logos and checks for updates over the
# web. lan: not needed — the brain routes inbound traffic.
permissions:
  internet: true
  lan: false

images:
  twentycrm/twenty:v2.13.2:
    digest: sha256:a2b79b8fd6fd5110617363438220d5aa4db7a8308769000ca8ab469918388307
    download_bytes: 242661907
    disk_bytes: 1186902016
apps.compose:
# Twenty adapted for malmo's Tier-3 sandbox (cap_drop: ALL, no-new-privileges,
# brain-pinned user:). Upstream's compose runs `server` + `worker` alongside a
# bundled postgres:16 and a redis, and publishes host port 3000. malmo drops the
# host port (the brain routes to main_port 3000), drops the two bundled
# datastores and declares managed Postgres + Redis (→ Valkey) in the manifest
# instead, and converts the shared `server-local-data` named volume to a
# relative ./data/local-storage bind — Twenty's STORAGE_TYPE=local attachment
# store, shared read/write by the server and the worker.
#
# Env rewiring (the brain injects the values; the compose maps them to Twenty's
# own names):
#   PG_DATABASE_URL  -> ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_DSN}  (managed Postgres 16)
#   REDIS_URL        -> ${MALMO_SERVICE_CACHE_DSN}      (managed Valkey 8; redis:// scheme)
#   SERVER_URL       -> ${MALMO_APP_URL}                (http://<slug>.local)
#   ENCRYPTION_KEY   -> ${MALMO_SECRET_ENCRYPTION_KEY}  (CSPRNG, generated once at install)
#
# The server runs DB migrations on boot (DISABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS left unset) and
# owns cron registration; the worker disables both (they already ran on the
# server) and drains the BullMQ job queue out of Redis. EMAIL_* and Google/
# Microsoft OAuth env are intentionally omitted. EMAIL_DRIVER is a boot-validated
# enum that defaults to `logger` (no-send) when unset, so Twenty boots clean and
# the admin configures SMTP in-app under Settings -> Emails (persisted to the DB).
# It can't be auto-wired from malmo's MALMO_MAIL_* provider: gating the enum to ""
# on an unbound box fails config validation at boot (see the PR's Platform gaps).
# OAuth SSO needs operator-supplied developer-app credentials malmo has no env
# surface for. Password + magic-link auth and the full CRM work without either.
services:
  server:
    image: twentycrm/twenty:v2.13.2
    environment:
      NODE_PORT: 3000
      SERVER_URL: ${MALMO_APP_URL}
      PG_DATABASE_URL: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_DSN}
      REDIS_URL: ${MALMO_SERVICE_CACHE_DSN}
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${MALMO_SECRET_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
      STORAGE_TYPE: local
    volumes:
      - ./data/local-storage:/app/packages/twenty-server/.local-storage:rw
    healthcheck:
      test: curl --fail http://localhost:3000/healthz
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 20
      # First boot runs schema migrations before the server listens (~55s on a
      # warm box, longer on the low-end hardware malmo targets). During the start
      # period failing probes don't count toward `retries`, so a slow migration
      # can't wedge the worker's depends_on: service_healthy gate. 150s matches
      # the manifest's 180s migration bound (250s total window vs 190s at 90s).
      start_period: 150s
    restart: unless-stopped

  worker:
    image: twentycrm/twenty:v2.13.2
    command: ["yarn", "worker:prod"]
    environment:
      SERVER_URL: ${MALMO_APP_URL}
      PG_DATABASE_URL: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_DSN}
      REDIS_URL: ${MALMO_SERVICE_CACHE_DSN}
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${MALMO_SECRET_ENCRYPTION_KEY}
      STORAGE_TYPE: local
      DISABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS: "true" # migrations run on the server
      DISABLE_CRON_JOBS_REGISTRATION: "true" # cron is registered on the server
    volumes:
      - ./data/local-storage:/app/packages/twenty-server/.local-storage:rw
    depends_on:
      server:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

apps.name: Unleash
apps.short_description: Self-hosted feature flags and progressive rollouts for your own apps.
apps.long_description:
Unleash is an open-source feature management platform that runs entirely on your own hardware. Wrap a risky change in a flag and turn it on or off at runtime without redeploying, target a feature at specific users or segments, roll it out gradually by percentage, and A/B test variants — all from one dashboard. Your own services connect to Unleash with an SDK and ask it which features are on, so flag state lives on your home server instead of a vendor cloud. malmo provisions and manages the PostgreSQL database Unleash needs automatically, so there is nothing to wire up.

Logging in the first time: Unleash starts with a single administrator account, username `admin`. Its initial password is generated uniquely for your install and shown on this app's page in malmo — copy it to sign in, then change it under your account settings. From there you can invite the rest of your household and create projects, environments, and flags.

Email is optional. Password resets, user invites, and the productivity report are delivered by email; until an administrator adds an outgoing email account in malmo and binds it to this app, those features quietly do nothing while everything else works. Note on links: Unleash builds invite and reset links from this app's local network address, so they only open from a device on your home network until remote access is available. The dashboard and your SDK clients work fully on your LAN today.

Wrap a risky change in a flag and turn it on or off at runtime without redeploying, target a feature at specific users or segments, roll it out gradually by percentage, and A/B test variants — all from one dashboard. Your own services connect to Unleash with an SDK and ask it which features are on, so flag state lives on your home server instead of a vendor cloud. malmo provisions and manages the PostgreSQL database Unleash needs automatically, so there is nothing to wire up.

compose_file: compose.yml
main_service: unleash

health_probe:
  path: /health
  start_period: 90s

# Unleash stores all feature-flag state in the managed Postgres database
# (backed up via the managed-service path). No local data binds.
storage:
  data_volumes: []
  app_managed_user_content: true

resources:
  recommended: { memory: 1G, cpu: 1.0 }

services:

    type: postgres

secrets:
  - name: unleash_secret
  - name: admin_password
    show: true

mail:
  optional: true

permissions:
  internet: true
  lan: false

images:
  unleashorg/unleash-server:8.0.1:
    digest: sha256:07139700dd09a68b35bac8b4b49456fbb1ec1bfb4cc80c360a1c2877dcbf9510
    download_bytes: 90111658
    disk_bytes: 328390144
apps.compose:
services:
  unleash:
    image: unleashorg/unleash-server:8.0.1
    environment:
      # Managed PostgreSQL (provisioned by malmo)
      DATABASE_HOST: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_HOST}
      DATABASE_PORT: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_PORT}
      DATABASE_NAME: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_NAME}
      DATABASE_USERNAME: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_USER}
      DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${MALMO_SERVICE_DATABASE_PASSWORD}
      DATABASE_SSL: "false"
      # Public URL: baked into invite/reset email links and the SDK connection URL
      UNLEASH_URL: ${MALMO_APP_URL}
      # Server secret (defaults to the insecure literal "super-secret" upstream)
      UNLEASH_SECRET: ${MALMO_SECRET_UNLEASH_SECRET}
      # Seed the single admin account on first boot with a generated, owner-visible password
      UNLEASH_DEFAULT_ADMIN_USERNAME: admin
      UNLEASH_DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD: ${MALMO_SECRET_ADMIN_PASSWORD}
      # No outbound version-check telemetry
      CHECK_VERSION: "false"
      # Optional outgoing email (no-op until an admin binds a mail provider in malmo)
      EMAIL_HOST: "${MALMO_MAIL_HOST:-}"
      EMAIL_PORT: "${MALMO_MAIL_PORT:-587}"
      EMAIL_USER: "${MALMO_MAIL_USER:-}"
      EMAIL_PASSWORD: "${MALMO_MAIL_PASSWORD:-}"
      EMAIL_SENDER: "${MALMO_MAIL_FROM:-}"
      EMAIL_SECURE: "${MALMO_MAIL_USE_SSL:-false}"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:4242/health"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 90s
